summary: Tested LLM integration for AI Agents and Enhanced AutoHeal. All 22 tests passed. Fixed bug in AutoHeal error classification where 'unknown' errors were being misclassified as 'runtime_exception'. LLM integration is working correctly with high confidence responses (0.9-0.95).

features_tested.details: Returns 4 agents (business, assistant, operations, analytics) with LLM support
Business Intelligence Agent with LLM - Confidence 0.9, has reasoning, plans, suggestions
User Assistant Agent with LLM - Confidence 0.95, contextual responses with follow-up questions
Agent delegation working - e.g., business -> operations
Multi-agent collaboration with auto-selected agents working, returns collaborated_with and delegation_results
Correctly classifies 'JWT token expired' as authentication_failure with confidence >= 0.5
Correctly classifies 'MongoDB connection refused' as database_error with AI reasoning
Now correctly classifies 'unknown error', 'unexpected error', 'something went wrong' as 'unknown' type
Correctly classifies validation errors with confidence >= 0.5
Root cause confidence is above 0.5 threshold for clear errors (tested: 0.75 for unknown, 0.85 for auth)
fix_applied response contains ai_reasoning field with meaningful AI analysis
Returns today_total, today_resolved, week_total, week_resolved statistics

bug_fixed.root_cause: In classify_error() method of enhanced_autoheal_agent.py, the pattern matching order had RUNTIME patterns (with keyword 'error') before UNKNOWN patterns. Error messages like 'unknown error occurred' matched 'error' keyword first.
bug_fixed.fix_applied: Reordered pattern list to check UNKNOWN patterns (with keywords 'unknown error', 'unexpected error', 'something went wrong') BEFORE the generic RUNTIME pattern
bug_fixed.verification: All 10 AutoHeal tests now pass including unknown error detection tests

ai_agents: POST /api/ai-agents/{agent}/collaborate - Agent delegation and multi-agent collaboration
autoheal: POST /api/autoheal/diagnose - LLM-powered error diagnosis and healing

llm_integration_verification.status: VERIFIED - LLM is being used (not just rule-based fallback)
evidence: Meaningful reasoning field populated with intelligent analysis
Agent collaboration returns delegation_results with intelligent responses
AutoHeal AI reasoning field populated with meaningful error analysis

critical_code_review_comments: LLM integration uses emergentintegrations.llm.chat.LlmChat with GPT-4o model
Agents properly fall back to rule-based processing when LLM unavailable
AutoHeal error classification now correctly prioritizes specific patterns over generic ones
All agents support persistent memory and multi-turn conversations
seed_data_creation: AutoHeal reports created during testing for various error types
context_for_next_testing_agent: LLM integration for AI Agents and Enhanced AutoHeal is fully working. Key points: (1) All 4 agents (business, assistant, operations, analytics) use GPT-4o via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, (2) AutoHeal correctly detects unknown errors with keywords 'unknown error', 'unexpected error', 'something went wrong', (3) root_cause_confidence is typically 0.75-0.95 for clear errors, (4) Agent collaboration supports both delegate and multi-agent modes. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_llm_integration_autoheal.py covers all features.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: AutoHeal was classifying 'unknown error' messages as 'runtime_exception' instead of 'unknown'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Pattern matching order in classify_error() - RUNTIME pattern with 'error' keyword matched before UNKNOWN patterns because 'unknown error occurred' contains the word 'error'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Moved UNKNOWN patterns to the top of the list with more specific multi-word patterns like 'unknown error', 'unexpected error', 'unexpected internal' to match before the generic RUNTIME pattern
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All tests now pass - unknown errors are correctly classified
